summary: Provision new Glacier vaults for long-term archival storage. Greenfield today (no vault exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the vault and its optional event notifications; a vault access policy and Vault Lock compliance policy are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new Glacier vault
operations.summary: Request a brand-new archival storage vault; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a Glacier vault as a complete specification: name and, optionally, event notifications. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: vault_name
params.label: Vault name
params.help: Names the vault in the Glacier console and the generated draft.

params.name: access_policy
params.label: Access policy
params.help: The vault's IAM access policy JSON is authored and reviewed by an engineer, never typed into this form. Leave blank for no vault-level policy.

params.name: notification_events
params.label: Notify on
params.help: Which vault events publish a notification. Leave blank for no notifications.

params.name: notification_sns_topic
params.label: Notification SNS topic
params.help: Required together with the events above — an engineer reconciles a half-filled notification config at review.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this vault archives
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this vault.

decisions: The vault access policy (IAM JSON) is authored and reviewed by an engineer, never captured from this form.
Vault Lock (aws_glacier_vault_lock, a one-way compliance policy) is a separate, deliberate engineer follow-up — never enabled by default.
